rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree blocks to contain AGREE syntax elements like assume, guarantee, eq, and assign.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE variables are grounded in visible AADL features or explicit declarations.
Use only valid AADL feature names and types when writing AGREE contracts.
Component type contracts must reference only visible ports and features at that scope level.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should be placed in appropriate AADL scopes: type for ports, implementation for subcomponents.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes unless they refer to valid visible outputs.
Avoid referencing non-existent or non-visible identifiers in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
